hypothesis: Mainline cost-reduction step on `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` with only `NUM_CODES` changed from the implicit default to `128`. Final reconstruction remains `x_hat = U(Vx) + Σ_m pi_m c_m + Σ_i alpha_i^(1) b_i + Σ_j alpha_j^(2) b_j`, so this family is still `扩展兼容`: LUTurbo/Lottable can export the low-rank trunk library, the static codebook library `C`, and the static residual dictionary `B`, then run `trunk lookup + codebook lookup + two sparse residual lookups + weighted sum` online. This round asks one question only: can a smaller coarse codebook preserve most of the EF=2 mainline quality while reducing both encoder selection cost and deployment lookup cost enough to improve the Pareto point. Reducing `NUM_CODES` directly shrinks `code_router`, `codebook_matmul`, and `codebook_deploy`, making it a cleaner first cost knob than changing multiple recipe terms at once.
next_hypotheses: If `NUM_CODES=128` holds FVU close to the current mainline while reducing cost, continue along the same axis with a smaller codebook or then test `TRUNK_RANK` as the next single cost knob.
If FVU degrades sharply, revert to the current mainline codebook size and try `TRUNK_RANK` instead as the next mainline cost-reduction variable.
notes_to_memory: `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` remains LUTurbo `扩展兼容` under `NUM_CODES=128`; only the size of the static coarse codebook changes, not the export path.

This round isolates the coarse-codebook-size question and should reduce both selection cost and deployment cost simultaneously, unlike `STAGE1_RATIO` which mostly reallocates sparse budget without shrinking static lookup structures.
Review `tokens_per_sec`, wall-clock time, and `final_total_tokens` together with FVU to confirm whether any improvement comes from true efficiency rather than a shorter or cheaper run artifact.
self_review: Single-variable discipline is preserved: the family and main recipe stay fixed, and only `NUM_CODES` changes. This follows the stated mainline priority order by keeping EF in-budget and then applying a direct cost knob that can improve both selector cost and LUTurbo deployment cost without introducing a new architecture.
summary: Push the mainline family forward by shrinking the coarse codebook to `NUM_CODES=128` while keeping `K=128`, `EF=2`, `signum`, and `lr=3e-3` fixed. This is a clean Pareto test for whether the current two-stage soft-codebook architecture can trade a smaller static coarse library for lower total LUTurbo cost without giving up too much reconstruction quality.
